snapshot: At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 59,004.83, down 513.51. Profit-taking after the prior day's surge is still dominating the index.
Prime-market breadth was 514 gainers, 1001 losers and 56 unchanged. Only 4 of the 33 sectors were up.
USD/JPY stayed in the lower 159s. The pair remains close enough to 160 for intervention risk to matter, but it did not break through in the morning.
Brent fell to $98.14 and WTI to $93.15. Oil has slipped back below $100, but the overall price level is still high.
The index is weak, yet capital is still rotating into clear stock-specific stories such as earnings beats, shareholder-return upgrades and thematic trades.

drivers.driver: Profit-taking after the prior day's 1,300-point surge
drivers.why_today: With the Nikkei just off record highs, short-term overheating is being unwound. By 11:00 JST the index was down 513 points, with Friday positioning adding to the profit-taking bias.

drivers.driver: Oil is lower than the panic phase, but still elevated, while USD/JPY remains in the low 159s
drivers.why_today: The panic tone has eased, but the combination of high oil and a weak yen has not disappeared. That makes broad-based risk appetite harder to sustain.

drivers.driver: Shareholder-return changes, earnings revisions and physical-AI themes are driving the relative winners
drivers.why_today: Data Applications, Asahi Diamond, Oro, Bandai Namco and Techno Horizon are all being lifted by specific, easy-to-explain catalysts rather than by a supportive index backdrop.

headline_inventory.headline: At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 59,004.83 (-513.51), with 514 gainers versus 1001 losers on the Prime market
As of 11:00 JST, only 4 of the 33 Prime-market sectors were trading higher
The BOJ governor avoided hinting at an April rate hike, sharply reducing hawkish market expectations
Asian markets saw Brent at $98.14 and WTI at $93.15, confirming crude had slipped back below $100
Japan's finance minister said the government was watching markets with an extremely high sense of urgency as oil futures spill over into FX
Data Applications shifted its financial policy and adopted a 100% total payout ratio target
Asahi Diamond revised full-year ordinary profit from a decline to a 10% increase
Oro announced cancellation of shares equal to 2.19% of shares outstanding
Bandai Namco Holdings announced cancellation of 5 million treasury shares
Datasection revised up its FY March 2026 profit forecast
Techno Horizon was locked at the daily limit-up bid as a physical-AI and robotics trade

watchlist.name: Data Applications
watchlist.why_today: The new 100% total payout ratio policy pushed the stock near limit-up levels. Among today's movers, this is the clearest shareholder-return rerating story.
watchpoints: Can it hold near the 1,000-yen area into the afternoon
Does the market keep rewarding the 100% payout policy
Can real dip-buying volume remain in place
falsifiers: Investors decide the policy is aspirational rather than immediately actionable
The stock gives back too much of the morning spike
watchlist.red_team: The return-policy message is strong, but it is still a framework change rather than an immediate cash action.

sources.title: データアプリ(3848) 中期経営計画における財務方針の変更および2026年3月期 剰余金の配当に関するお知らせ

注目銘柄ダイジェスト（前場）:旭ダイヤ、WHDC、データアプリなど

watchlist.name: Asahi Diamond Industrial
watchlist.why_today: The operating-profit upgrade flipped the company back to a growth profile, and the stock still held a gain of more than 9% in the morning.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 1,300-yen area into the afternoon
Does the market keep rewarding the operating-profit upside
Can it absorb concern over the impairment charge
falsifiers: The impairment loss becomes the dominant read
The stock cannot regain the morning high zone
watchlist.red_team: The earnings picture is better, but the special-loss element still leaves room for pushback on quality.

sources.title: 旭ダイヤ(6140) 連結業績予想の修正および特別損失（固定資産の減損損失）の計上に関するお知らせ
旭ダイヤが急速人気化、半導体向け好調で２６年３月期営業利益上方修正で一転増益に
旭ダイヤ---大幅続伸、前期経常利益は一転2ケタ増予想に上方修正

watchlist.name: Techno Horizon
watchlist.why_today: The stock is pinned at a limit-up bid on physical-AI and robotics speculation. Even without fresh hard numbers, the intraday priority is high because the move is so strong.
watchpoints: Can it keep the 1,070-yen limit-up bid into the afternoon
Does capital stay in the physical-AI theme
Can it avoid a sudden reversal once the bid loosens
falsifiers: The limit-up bid disappears and the stock quickly loses altitude
The theme is treated as too speculative to survive the afternoon
watchlist.red_team: This is still a theme-led trade with limited fresh numeric backing, so the afternoon volatility risk is high.

sources.title: Ｔホライゾンがストップ高カイ気配、ＡＩ・ロボティクス融合で商機獲得に期待
＜動意株・１７日＞（前引け）＝Tホライゾン、旭ダイヤ、データアプリ

watchlist.name: Oro
watchlist.why_today: The company announced cancellation of shares equal to 2.19% of the share count. The market is reacting in a straightforward way, with the stock still up in the 3% range.
watchpoints: Does the cancellation keep attracting buyers after the open
Can turnover remain active enough for continuation
falsifiers: The stock fades into a simple sell-the-news reaction
Investors decide the March buyback already priced in much of the effect
watchlist.red_team: The supply-improvement story is clear, but smaller-cap follow-through can still become unstable quickly.

watchlist.name: Bandai Namco Holdings
watchlist.why_today: The company announced cancellation of 5 million treasury shares. The reaction is milder than in smaller names, but the story remains easy to track in a weak market.
watchpoints: Can it keep trading around the 3,970-yen area into the afternoon
Does the market keep rewarding the cancellation cleanly
Can it retain stock-specific strength inside the entertainment group
falsifiers: The market decides the cancellation is already priced in
Large-cap profit-taking dominates the afternoon tape
watchlist.red_team: The catalyst is clear, but large-cap names often deliver only limited range expansion after the first response.

deep_dive.title: Data Applications: the key afternoon question is whether the 100% payout-ratio story can keep carrying

lines: The core of the move is the clarity of the new capital-allocation message: a 100% total payout ratio target.
On a day when the index is weak, a straightforward shareholder-return policy shift can create powerful relative strength in a smaller-cap name.
At 16:00 JST on April 16, the company disclosed a change in financial policy that added a 100% total payout ratio target and an active stance toward buybacks.
By the April 17 morning session, the stock had climbed close to limit-up territory and was still highlighted in the late-morning market recap.
Can it hold around the 1,000-yen area and re-test the morning high zone in the afternoon.
Does real dip-buying volume keep the move from becoming only an opening spike.
Investors decide the policy shift is more of a long-term target than an actionable near-term trigger.
The stock gives back too much of the morning spike and turns into a pure one-session volatility trade.
Because this is a smaller-cap stock, profit-taking can destabilize the move quickly.
If the broader market turns back into an index-led selloff, even a strong shareholder-return story may lose traction.

sources.title: データアプリ(3848) 中期経営計画における財務方針の変更および2026年3月期 剰余金の配当に関するお知らせ

no_trade_conditions: If the Nikkei gives back most of the 11:00 JST rebound and turns decisively lower again
If USD/JPY accelerates back toward 160 and both intervention fear and import-cost pressure return at the same time
If Brent or WTI spike again and reverse the improving risk tone
If the top catalyst names such as Data Applications and Asahi Diamond fail to hold their morning high zones and break lower on real volume
content.disclaimer: This is a midday market digest, not investment advice. Please make your own final decisions.
